operating_state: Total revenue increased $2.1 billion to $19.3 billion driven by organic growth at Pratt & Whitney from higher GTF volume and military sustainment, Collins from commercial aerospace aftermarket recovery and OEM volume increases, and Raytheon from land and air defense systems programs including international Patriot and C-UAS. Commercial aerospace aftermarket sales strengthened across segments reflecting increased flight hours and air traffic recovery.
cost_structure: Total cost of sales increased $2.1 billion to $15.7 billion with organic increases tracking revenue growth. Gross margin declined to 18.4% from 20.7% primarily due to $175 million in charges at Collins for unfavorable titanium purchase commitments and contract fulfillment cost impairments from Canadian sanctions on Russian suppliers. Operating margin remained stable at 9.7% despite inflation and supply chain pressures.
material_events: Raytheon completed the sale of its Cybersecurity, Intelligence and Services business for approximately $1.3 billion generating a $0.4 billion gain. The company repaid $950 million of notes due 2024 in March and an additional $500 million term loan in April. Phil Jasper was appointed president of Raytheon effective January 7, replacing Wesley Kremer who retired.
macro_exposures: Canadian government sanctions on Russian-owned titanium suppliers resulted in $175 million in charges at Collins for alternative sourcing arrangements. China announced sanctions against Data Link Solutions LLC, a Collins joint venture, adding to existing sanctions against other RTX entities. Contract liabilities include $405 million in advance payments from a Middle East customer where regulatory approvals are no longer expected.
forward_outlook: Management expects continued supply chain disruptions and inflationary pressures on material, component, and labor costs. The company estimates approximately $1.3 billion in cash outflows for the Powder Metal Matter in 2024 for customer compensation tied to accelerated PW1100 engine inspections. RTX maintains $1.0 billion remaining authority under its share repurchase program.
uncertainty_profile: The Powder Metal Matter requires accelerated inspection of the PW1100 GTF fleet with timing of customer compensation cash flows subject to fleet management plan execution variables. Geopolitical tensions including the Israel-Hamas conflict and potential additional China sanctions create operational uncertainties. Pending regulatory approvals for foreign military sales remain subject to government policy changes and approval timing.
